message:
test: comprehensive test expansions for lifecycle, SDK, manifest, registry, and ratelimit

Add ~2400 lines of new unit tests across five crates to dramatically improve coverage
of error paths, edge cases, and trait implementations:

- fcp-core/lifecycle: +1042 lines covering the complete invalid state transition matrix
  (every forbidden state→state pair), idempotent transitions, policy cloning, and error
  Display formatting for all LifecycleError variants
- fcp-sdk/lib: +315 lines testing SchemaValidator clone/debug, validation error Display
  and path formatting, format_schema_errors with various error counts, and edge cases
  for empty/nested schemas
- fcp-sdk/retry: +442 lines covering RetryDecision variants (is_retryable, retry_after),
  RetryConfig builder patterns, backoff delay computation, max_retries boundary,
  classify_status_code for HTTP codes, and Debug/Clone/Eq derives
- fcp-registry/lib: +273 lines testing RegistryError Display for all variants (including
  MissingSignatures, UnknownKid, SignatureInvalid, PublisherThresholdUnmet, etc.)
- fcp-manifest/lib: +302 lines covering ConnectorArchetype as_str/serde/derive,
  ConnectorRuntimeFormat serde/debug/copy, ManifestError Display variants, and
  endpoint validation edge cases

Also fix existing ratelimit tests:
- backoff: use f64::EPSILON for float comparisons instead of ==, fix jitter bound
  assertion (max 90s with 0.5 jitter factor), use deterministic no-jitter in clone_box test
- fcp: rename underscore-prefixed permit variables, use Duration::from_secs(1) instead
  of from_millis(1000)
- headers: use idiomatic contains_key() instead of .get().is_none()
- token_bucket: relax reset_after assertion to bounded check since refill timing is
  non-deterministic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
